violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to register a food safety manager with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Proof of registration is to be submitted to inspector. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to register a food safety manager with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen and bar area was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were provided during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Raw animal foods (such as beef patties) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as bread and vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were rearranged during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Chowder was observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food item was held for less than 2 hours prior to time of inspection and was reheated to 174 degrees Fahrenheit during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Burgers are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Salad mix and beef patties were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Food items were moved onto shelving within unit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The door to both of the establishment's toilet rooms is not self-closing. Toilet room doors that open to food preparation spaces shall be self-closing to limit potential contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2025
